raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/XXXX, I reported XXXX unauthorized charges on my GM Rewards Mastercard. I was told by the card issuer that the transactions would be reviewed and removed. Based on that information, I closed the account. 

I did not authorize these transactions. 

In XX/XX/XXXX, I received a notice stating that my payment was late for the disputed charges and that a {$30.00} late fee had been added. This was the first time I was made aware that the charges were not removed. 

After contacting the company again, I was told that my fraud dispute had been denied, allegedly because the card was in my possession and XXXX chip technology was used. I was informed that letters were sent on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, but I was not clearly notified in a way that required action, nor was I warned that the balance would age or incur penalties. 

The company : Denied my fraud claim without meaningful explanation Failed to ensure clear, actionable notice Allowed a disputed balance to become past due Added a late fee improperly Potentially exposed me to negative credit reporting Regardless of the transaction method used, I did not authorize the charges. Chip usage does not prove authorization. Additionally, penalties should not be applied to balances under active dispute, particularly when the account was closed based on the companys representations.
